retrieved_docs.title: Impetigo -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: In areas of a high prevalence of MRSA  or if cultures are positive for MRSA, clindamycin or doxycycline are the preferred treatments. Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole is effective against MRSA, but should only be used if group A streptococci are not the causative agent, or in addition to an anti-streptococcal antibiotic.

retrieved_docs.title: Cephalexin -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Cephalexin is an antibiotic approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in 1970 and is classified as a first-generation cephalosporin. This drug is widely used in outpatient and inpatient healthcare settings due to its favorable safety and efficacy profile. Cephalexin is commonly utilized in the treatment of urinary tract infections, respiratory infections, otitis media, bone infections caused by Staphylococcus aureus or Proteus mirabilis , and skin and soft tissue infections primarily caused by S aureus or Streptococcus pyogenes . Cephalexin is administered prophylactically to minimize the risk of surgical site infections and other bacterial infections. Both streptococcal and staphylococcal species can cause these infections. This comprehensive activity provides an overview of the indications, mechanism of action, contraindications, and adverse effects of cephalexin, enabling the interprofessional healthcare team to utilize it appropriately in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: Methicillin-Resistant Staphylococcus aureus  -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Describe the recommended management of MRSA Outline the typical presentation for a patient with MRSA. Review the pathophysiology of MRSA. Outline the importance of enhancing care coordination among the interprofessional team to ensure proper evaluation and management of a MRSA infection. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.content: Alternative regimens are a first-generation cephalosporin, e.g., cefazolin 2 g IV every 8 hours, clindamycin 900 mg IV every 8 hours, vancomycin 15 mg/kg IV every 12 hours, oxacillin or nafcillin 2 g IV every 4 hours

retrieved_docs.title: Beta-Lactam Antibiotics -- Indications -- Cephalosporins
retrieved_docs.content: First-generation cephalosporins Cefazolin(IV), cephalexin (PO), cefadroxil (PO) Skin and soft tissue infections serious infections due to MSSA Perioperative surgical prophylaxis Second-generation cephalosporins Cefuroxime (IV/PO), cefoxitin (IV), cefotetan (IV), cefaclor (PO) cefprozil (PO) Upper respiratory tract infections (sinusitis, otitis media) Cefoxitin, cefotetan-gynecologic infections, perioperative surgical prophylaxis Third-generation cephalosporins Cefotaxime (IV), ceftriaxone (IV), cefpodoxime (PO), cefixime (PO), cefdinir (PO), cefditoren (PO), ceftibuten (PO) Community-acquired pneumonia, meningitis Urinary tract infections Streptococcal endocarditis Gonorrhea Severe Lyme disease. Anti-pseudomonal Cephalosporins Ceftazidime (IV), ceftazidime/avibactam (IV), cefepime (IV) [Fourth-generation], ceftolozone/tazobactam (IV) [also been described as "fifth-generation"]

retrieved_docs.title: Methicillin-Resistant Staphylococcus aureus  -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: The selection of empiric antibiotic therapy for the treatment of MRSA infection depends on the type of disease, local S. aureus resistance patterns, availability of the drug, side effect profile, and individual patient profile. [11] [12] [13]

retrieved_docs.title: Methicillin-Resistant Staphylococcus aureus  -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Based on the antibiotic susceptibilities, Methicillin resistance in S. aureus is defined as an oxacillin minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of greater than or equal to 4 micrograms/mL. MRSA infection is one of the leading causes of hospital-acquired infections and is commonly associated with significant morbidity, mortality, length of stay, and cost burden. MRSA infections can be further divided into hospital-associated (HA-MRSA) infections and community-associated (CA-MRSA) infections. They differ not only in respect to their clinical features and molecular biology but also to their antibiotic susceptibility and treatment. This activity reviews the evaluation and management of MRSA and highlights the role of the interprofessional team in the recognition and management of this condition.

retrieved_docs.title: Osteomyelitis (Nursing) -- Medical Management -- Staphylococcus aureus methicillin-resistant
retrieved_docs.content: Treatment of choice is vancomycin IV An alternative regimen is linezolid 600 mg IV every 12 hours.

retrieved_docs.title: Cefuroxime -- Mechanism of Action -- Susceptible Organisms
retrieved_docs.content: Notably, cefuroxime is generally ineffective against most strains of enterococci (eg, Enterococcus faecalis ). In addition, the medication does not effectively target methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) and Listeria monocytogenes .

retrieved_docs.title: Vancomycin -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the mechanism of action of vancomycin. Determine the FDA-approved indications and off-label uses for vancomycin therapy. Assess the potential adverse effects associated with vancomycin administration. Determine the importance of collaboration and coordination among interprofessional team members for enhancing patient care when administering and monitoring vancomycin therapy. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 4 -- Community 2715
retrieved_docs.content: Minimum bactericidal concentration (MBC) is a quantitative measure that defines the lowest concentration of an antimicrobial agent required to kill a bacterial population, and it is directly related to the antibiotic framycetin. Understanding the MBC of framycetin helps clinicians assess the drug’s bactericidal potency, guide appropriate dosing, and evaluate its effectiveness against specific bacterial infections, thereby informing optimal patient care and antimicrobial stewardship.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1430
retrieved_docs.content: Cefaklor and paromomycin each fulfill an antibacterial therapeutic role, serving as antibiotics that are employed to treat bacterial infections. Their shared function highlights their importance in antimicrobial therapy and underscores their relevance in managing bacterial diseases.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 2028
retrieved_docs.content: Anisomycin, monensin, cefotetan, paromomycin and puromycin are all classified as antibiotics. The category “antibiotika” encompasses these agents, with cefotetan, paromomycin and puromycin identified as child members within this group, while anisomycin and monensin are directly recognized as antibiotics. Understanding that each of these compounds belongs to the antibiotic class guides their appropriate use in antimicrobial therapy and informs clinical decision‑making regarding infection treatment.
